title: Flexible heart valve prosthesis
abstract: A flexible heart valve prosthetic device includes a flexible frame having a plurality of struts disposed radially around a central axis and defining a space configured to accept entry of a native anterior leaflet to an interior portion of the frame including the central axis. A mesh band is attached to each of the plurality of struts between their proximal ends and their distal ends. A leaflet is at least partially radially disposed within the mesh band and configured to mate with the native anterior leaflet. A method of releasably anchoring a flexible heart valve prosthetic within the annulus of the heart of a subject is also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A flexible heart valve prosthetic device, comprising: a flexible frame comprising a plurality of struts disposed radially around a central axis, each strut joined at a proximal end and a distal end; a mesh band attached to each strut between their proximal ends and their distal ends; a deployable anchoring assembly comprising a deployable hook positioned on each strut within the mesh band; a retrieval hook positioned at the proximal and/or distal ends of the plurality of struts; and a plurality of leaflets radially disposed within the mesh band. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the plurality of struts of the deployable frame deploys away from the central axis of the device. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the struts of the deployable frame define a space configured to accept entry of a native anterior leaflet to an interior portion of the frame including the central axis. 4. The device of claim 1, wherein the deployable frame is constructed from a non-ferromagnetic, flexible material. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the non-ferromagnetic, flexible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 6 The device of claim 1, wherein the deployable frame comprises a coating with an anti -thrombolytic or immunosuppressant agent. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the mesh band comprises a plurality of fibers. 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the mesh band comprises a plurality of leaflet struts. 9. The device of claim 8, where the leaflet struts are constructed from non- ferromagnetic, flexible material. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the non-ferromagnetic, flexible material is is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 11. The device of claim 8, where the leaflet struts are constructed from flexible material such as polyethylene, polyester, nylon, PTFE, and ePTFE. 12. The device of claim 7, wherein the mesh band is constructed from a non ferromagnetic biocompatible material. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein the non-ferromagnetic biocompatible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 14. The device of claim 1, wherein the plurality of deployable hooks is constructed from a non -ferromagnetic biocompatible material. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein the non-ferromagnetic biocompatible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 16. The device of claim 1, wherein the retrieval hook is constructed from a non-ferromagnetic biocompatible material. 17. The device of claim 16, wherein the non-ferromagnetic biocompatible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 18. The device of claim 1, wherein the plurality of leaflets comprises two or three leaflets. 19. The device of claim 18, where the plurality of leaflets is constructed from a biological material or a synthetic material. 20. The device of claim 19, wherein the biological material is preserved biological tissue collected from a donor wherein the donor species is selected from the group consisting of human, bovine, equine, and porcine. 21. The device of claim 19, wherein the synthetic material is a fiber-reinforced matrix material. 22. A method of releasably anchoring a flexible heart valve prosthetic within the annulus of the heart of a subject, the method comprising: inserting a valve prosthetic removal device through the vasculature into the heart of the subject, the device comprising a fixed elongated member with a loop or hook and a reducing member; threading the loop or hook of the elongated member through the retrieval hook of the prosthetic of claim 1; withdrawing the prosthetic into the reducing member by way of applying tension to the retrieval hook; and removing the prosthetic device from the heart through the vasculature of the subject. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein tension upon the retrieval hook reversibly retracts the plurality of deployable hooks whereby releasing the device from its anchored position. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein tension upon the retrieval hook reversibly compresses the device into a tubular conformation. 25. A flexible heart valve prosthetic device, comprising: a flexible frame comprising a plurality of struts disposed radially around a central axis, a first and second strut of the plurality of struts defining an entry space for a native anterior leaflet; a mesh band extending partially or fully around the circumference of the flexible frame; a retrieval hook positioned at the proximal and/or distal ends of the plurality of struts; a deployable anchoring assembly comprising a deployable hook positioned on each strut within the mesh band; and a leaflet disposed within the flexible frame and configured to mate with the native anterior leaflet. 26. A flexible heart valve prosthetic device comprising: a flexible frame comprising a plurality of struts disposed radially around a central axis and defining a space configured to accept entry of a native anterior leaflet to an interior portion of the frame including the central axis; a mesh band attached to each of the plurality of struts between their proximal ends and their distal ends; and a leaflet at least partially radially disposed within the mesh band and configured to mate with the native anterior leaflet. 27. The device of claim 1 further comprising: 

 at least one strut having a length shorter than one or more of the plurality of struts, the at least one strut at least partially defining the space configured to accept entry of the native anterior leaflet. 28. The device of claim 1 further comprising: at least one strut having a structure different than one or more of the plurality of struts, the at least one strut at least partially defining the space configured to accept entry of the native anterior leaflet. 29. The device of claim 1 further comprising: a bend in two or more of the plurality of struts to at least partially define the space configured to accept entry of the native anterior leaflet. 30. The device of claim 1 further comprising: a first spacing between two or more of the plurality of struts that is larger than a second spacing between two or more of the remaining plurality of struts to at least partially define the space configured to accept entry of the native anterior leaflet. 31. The device of claim 26 further comprising: a deployable anchoring assembly comprising a deployable hook positioned on each of the plurality of struts within the mesh band. 32. The device of claim 26 further comprising: a retrieval hook positioned at the proximal and/or distal ends of the plurality of struts. 33. The device of claim 26, wherein the plurality of struts deploys away from the central axis of the device. 34. The device of claim 26, wherein the plurality of struts are constructed from a non-ferromagnetic, flexible material. 35. The device of claim 34, wherein the non-ferromagnetic, flexible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 36. The device of claim 26, wherein the plurality of struts comprises a coating with an anti -thrombolytic or immunosuppressant agent. 37. The device of claim 26, wherein the mesh band comprises a plurality of fibers. 38. The device of claim 26, wherein the mesh band comprises a leaflet strut. 39. The device of claim 38, where the leaflet strut is constructed from non- ferromagnetic, flexible material. 40. The device of claim 26, wherein the mesh band is constructed from a non ferromagnetic biocompatible material. 41. The device of claim 40, wherein the non-ferromagnetic biocompatible material is selected from the group consisting of: a chromium alloy, stainless steel, a titanium alloy, and Nitinol. 42. The device of claim 26, where the leaflet is constructed from a biological material or a medical grade synthetic material. 43. The device of claim 42, wherein the biological material is preserved biological tissue collected from a donor wherein the donor species is selected from the group consisting of human, bovine, equine, and porcine. 44. The device of claim 42, wherein the medical grade synthetic material is a fiber-reinforced matrix material.
